data.title: CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) Jumps 13% After CEO Responds to Critics
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) stock was up 13% on Jan. 12 after CEO Michael Intrator responded to criticisms about the company's business model.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Speaking on the Big Technology Podcast, Intrator shot down arguments about the useful life of graphics processing units (GPUs) that are used in artificial intelligence (AI) data centers, a concern that has weighed on the stocks of AI infrastructure companies such as CoreWeave.

CRWV stock is also up after a Moody's Ratings (MCO) report projected that AI data center investments will top $3 trillion over the next five years. The ratings firm identified CoreWeave among six U.S. companies expected to collectively invest $500 billion in data centers this year alone.

CoreWeave's Partnership with Nvidia

CoreWeave also recently announced plans to add Nvidia's (NVDA) Rubin AI microchip and related hardware to its AI cloud-computing platform in the second half of this year, positioning it among the first cloud providers to deploy the platform for customers building and running large-scale AI workloads.

Some analysts have criticized CoreWeave and its business model that's focused on developing AI data centers and then renting them out to other companies, mostly technology hyperscalers. But in a new note, investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) said that CoreWeave's "purpose-built architecture" provides "a significant competitive advantage" for the company.

Is CRWV Stock a Buy?

CoreWeave's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 25 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 12 Buy and 13 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average CRWV price target of $123.23 implies 36.22% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: "Going Big Time Into 14A," Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slides on New Pivot
data.description:
Chip stock Intel (INTC) only recently got the 18A node fired up, and there are some signs that it is doing good work already. But Intel is not resting on its laurels and giving 18A room to grow. Rather, it is already putting a lot of resources and effort behind the 14A node. There is something to be said for aggressive development, but investors may be concerned about too much change too quickly. They evidenced that concern by sending Intel shares down nearly 3% in Monday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Intel used its presence at this year's Consumer Electronics Show (CES) event to introduce the Core Ultra 3 processors, a major part of the 18A line. But Intel did not seem to focus on the 18A line very long before also using its presence at CES to talk about the 14A process. Lip-Bu Tan himself noted that the process is coming along nicely, and offered up some specifics on just what the 14A process is doing and can do.

This was an eyebrow-raiser for many, especially those who remembered Tan's earlier statements about pulling the plug on the whole process if it did not land a client. But Tan turned it right around, saying, "We are going big time into 14A. Stay tuned, we are going to see a lot of great momentum on the 14A in terms of yields and IP portfolio to serve the customer well." With 14A set to go live in 2027, at last report, it already represents a surprising leap forward.

An Unexpected Comparison

Then came an unexpected comparison that Intel should benefit from. The Panther Lake lineup is actually testing well against Sony's (SONY) PS6 handheld, though at slightly different wattages. The current word suggests that the Panther Lake handhelds will perform similarly to the PS6, currently running under the code name "Canis." The one downside is that the Panther Lake chips will need 30 watts of power to run, while Sony's chips will need just 15.

Much of that discrepancy is in the operating system. Sony runs a proprietary system through its Canis system, while Panther Lake will not benefit from such optimization. Still, for Panther Lake chips to be on par with next-gen gaming systems suggests good news for Intel to come, even if, perhaps, battery life might not be ideal on a Panther Lake-powered handheld device.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on five Buys, 19 Holds and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 137.24% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $39.09 per share implies 11.81% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: 'Don't Bet On It,' Says Top Investor About Tesla Stock
data.description:
On one level, the optimism around Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) stock is pretty easy to understand. After all, the company's big bets on autonomous driving and humanoid robots are rooted in the pursuit of potentially trillions of dollars in future opportunities.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In that light, drooping EV sales in the here and now haven't raised too many alarm bells. TSLA's share price continues to enjoy high valuation premiums – ones that are head and shoulders above those of other automobile manufacturers – even after two straight years of falling deliveries.

Tesla's latest Q4 deliveries update is a prime example of this phenomenon. Deliveries slid 15.6% year-over-year to 418,227 vehicles, yet the stock remains comfortably above $440 – a sign that investors are still willing to look past today's delivery slowdown and bet that Elon Musk's long-term autonomy vision will ultimately outweigh current fundamentals.

However, top investor Daniel Sparks isn't so confident such a lucrative future awaits the company and its investors.

"What if Tesla's Robotaxi service turns out to be a costly and commoditized business?" asks the 5-star investor, who is among the top 1% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Sparks lays out two main concerns. The first revolves around Tesla's capital expenditures, which were close to $9 billion in 2025. The investor cites recent statements from the company's CFO, who acknowledged that Tesla's capex is expected to "increase substantially" in 2026. Sparks worries that these costs could balloon even higher than investors are expecting.

The second issue the investor raises is the profitability of the future robotaxis, which Sparks points out is going to be competing in "a highly competitive, price-sensitive market." He notes that large tech giants (Alphabet and Amazon), EV makers (Rivian, Lucid, and BYD), and existing ride-sharing companies (Uber and Lyft) are all racing to offer their own autonomous driving services.

In other words, rather than pushing the boundaries of technological advancements, the full self-driving market could find itself turning into something resembling a commodity. Viewed in that light, the margins might not be all that attractive.

"Intuitively, a taxi service boils down to price when customers select their rides. So, it may be difficult for one company to differentiate itself on any factor other than price," adds Sparks.

Ultimately, that doesn't leave enough margin for Sparks to feel comfortable with an investment in Tesla.

"I believe the bull case for Robotaxi is largely priced in, and the bear case is mostly ignored," concludes Sparks. (To watch Sparks' track record, click here)

Wall Street's stance reflects that tension. While 13 analysts are willing to give Elon Musk the benefit of the doubt, 10 remain on the sidelines and 7 recommend selling, resulting in a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Meanwhile, the Street's 12-month average price target of $405.94 implies a downside of high single digits from current levels. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Trump Says U.S. is 'SCREWED' if Supreme Court Overturns Tariffs
data.description:
President Trump has warned that the U.S. would be subject to "Hundreds of Billions of Dollars" of refunds if the Supreme Court rules against his tariffs. The legal precedent of the tariffs hinges on the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), which allows the president to impose tariffs to address national emergencies.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"In other words, if the Supreme Court rules against the United States of America on this National Security bonanza, WE'RE SCREWED!" Trump said in a Truth Social post on Monday.

Trump Administration Prepares Tariff Backup Plan

Trump added that the payback amount would surge to the trillion-dollar level when including the investments made by companies to sidestep tariffs.

The Supreme Court held an opinion day last Friday, although it didn't provide a decision on the tariff case. The next earliest date that SCOTUS could issue a ruling is on Wednesday, January 14. Last week, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said that the Trump administration has already begun looking into legal alternatives to justify the tariffs, such as Section 301 and Section 122 of the Trade Act.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Israel's Teva Pharmaceuticals (TEVA) Pops on Upbeat Guidance
data.description:
The stock of Israel's Teva Pharmaceutical Industries (TEVA) was up as much as 4% on Jan. 12 after the generic drug maker issued strong guidance for the year ahead.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company, which is headquartered in Tel Aviv, said that its full-year earnings per share (EPS) will be at the higher end of its $2.55 to $2.65 guidance when it reports fourth-quarter financial results on Jan. 28. The top end of that range is above the earnings of $2.63 per share expected on Wall Street.

Additionally, Teva Pharmaceutical's 2026 guidance and targets for 2027 are higher than the company had previously forecast, giving the company's share price a boost. Over the last 12 months, TEVA stock has risen nearly 60%, making it a top-performing stock.

Teva's Bullish Outlook and Famous Shareholders

Management at Teva Pharmaceutical said they expect 2026 revenue to be between flat and slightly down compared with last year, while forecasting low-single digit growth for 2027. Wall Street is currently predicting around a 0.5% fall in revenue in 2026 and a 2.5% increase the next year.

The company added that it expects operating profit and adjusted earnings to grow compared to 2025 and jump in 2027, with operating profit up 30% next year and rising another 30% by 2030. TEVA stock counts some notable shareholders, including famed investors Stanley Druckenmiller and David Einhorn.

Is TEVA Stock a Buy?

Teva stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 10 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average TEVA price target of $35.67 implies 9.32% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Wavers on DOJ Probe of Powell as JPMorgan Warns of 'Near-Term Underperformance'
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading slightly lower on Monday as investors digest the implications of the Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Over the weekend, trump">Powell announced that he was subject to a probe over the Fed's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. However, he believes the true reason for the investigation relates to the Fed setting interest rates independently and without political bias.

"The threat of criminal charges is a consequence of the Federal Reserve setting interest rates based on our best assessment of what will serve the public, rather than following the preferences of the President," Powell said in a statement.

President Trump has repeatedly called for lower interest rates while criticizing Powell for delaying rate cuts. That's led to concern that the autonomy and independence of the central bank are at risk, with several former Fed Chairs and Treasury Secretaries addressing this issue in a joint statement on Monday. "The reported criminal inquiry into Federal Reserve Chair Jay Powell is an unprecedented attempt to use prosecutorial attacks to undermine that independence," read the statement.

The investigation into Powell also creates an "overhang" for stocks that could result in "near-term underperformance" as investors react to the risk of waning Fed independence, said Andrew Tyler, JPMorgan's Global Head of Market Intelligence. Tyler added that a de-escalation of threats to the Fed's independence could create a buying opportunity given that the economy and corporate fundamentals remain healthy.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.14% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Time to Hop In,' Says Investor About AMD Stock
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) shares have not been immune to mounting fears of an AI bubble, which have weighed on many of the market's former high-flyers after a strong stretch through much of 2025. After hitting an all-time high in October, AMD shares are now down about 21%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In fact, much of that decline has unfolded since the company's Q3 2025 earnings report, a move that appears somewhat surprising given that the company's underlying performance has remained strong.

According to one 5-star investor, known by the pseudonym RI Research, the recent weakness in AMD's share price reflects sentiment rather than substance. In the investor's view, AMD's fundamentals "remain rock-solid, while there are also accelerating AI-related tailwinds."

The numbers back up that confidence. AMD topped expectations on both the top and bottom lines in Q3, posting a 5.6% revenue beat alongside a 2.5% upside surprise on earnings. Revenue growth accelerated to 35.6% year over year, improving meaningfully from the 31.7% pace reported in Q2. Moreover, despite growing capex spending, the company's net cash position improved by some $1.5 billion in the latest quarter.

AMD is one of the biggest beneficiaries of the global AI data center spending. This trend appears primed to continue, declares RI, as the largest technology companies are all increasing their AI capex in 2026.

Moreover, these spending projects are neither one-off nor short-term, states the investor. The long-term planning required gives a "picks and shovels" company like AMD strong revenue and EPS visibility going forward.

Don't just take RI's word for it, either. The investor namechecks a couple of Wall Street pros who have made AMD one of their top picks for 2026.

Though competition is the "major risk" facing AMD, and Nvidia remains the undisputed data center leader, AMD's FY2028 forward price-to-earnings ratio below 15x is just too compelling for the investor.

So, for RI, the verdict is crystal clear: "AMD is dirt cheap at the moment." On that basis, the investor rates AMD shares a Strong Buy. (To watch RI Research's track record, click here)

Wall Street isn't pushing back on that view. With 28 Buy recommendations versus 9 Holds, AMD earns a Moderate Buy consensus rating. The Street's 12-month average price target of $283.34 suggests there's ~36% upside on the table from here. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Pulls Back: What Are the Next Catalysts for Investors in 2026?
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) shares have started 2026 on a softer note as investors reassess AI demand, valuation, and near-term growth. The stock has slipped about 3% over the past five trading days after CES headlines on robotics and autonomous driving failed to spark a rally. Adding to the caution, a surge in Chinese AI stocks—and growing speculation that China's tech sector could outperform U.S. peers by 2026—has weighed on sentiment. Year to date, NVDA is down 2.69%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Looking ahead, the focus turns to potential catalysts that could drive NVDA's next move, including new chip launches, data-center expansion, and signals from China. Approval of H200 shipments could provide an upside boost, though regulatory uncertainty remains a key overhang. While sustained AI demand and data-center growth support the long-term bull case, Nvidia's premium valuation and rising competition continue to pose risks.

Upcoming Catalysts for NVDA Stock in 2026

A key catalyst for the stock could be signs that Chinese companies are moving ahead with purchases of Nvidia's H200 chips. CEO Jensen Huang said at CES that demand for the China-focused chip is "very strong" and that he does not expect problems from regulators.

According to Reuters, Nvidia already has orders for more than two million H200 chips, priced at about $27,000 each—equal to roughly $54 billion in potential revenue. However, Chinese authorities have asked some tech firms to temporarily pause orders while they review how many domestically made chips should be bought alongside Nvidia's products.

The latest approval by the Trump administration unexpectedly gave Chinese companies access to the H200 chip, which is far more powerful than the previously approved H20. For many Chinese firms, the H200 is now the most advanced chip they can legally buy. However, shipments to China are not guaranteed. Chinese regulators have not yet formally approved imports of the H200 chip, and sources say officials are holding urgent discussions to review its potential impact.

In addition, investors may get a clearer view of the AI chip market this week when Nvidia's key supplier, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, or TSMC, (TSM), reports earnings on Thursday. The results could act as another catalyst for NVDA stock.

What Is the Target Price of Nvidia Stock?

On Wall Street, top analysts remain confident in Nvidia's core AI chip business, pointing to strong demand from a wide range of customers and its dominant market position.

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $264.97, Nvidia's average share price target implies a 43.34% upside potential.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Prepare for Zero Rate Cuts in 2026, Says JPMorgan
data.description:
A resilient labor market and the risk of inflation returning have swayed JPMorgan to forecast zero Fed rate cuts in 2026. In addition, the firm expects the central bank to raise rates by 25 bps during the third quarter of 2027.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The December labor report eased concerns about a slackening jobs market," said JPMorgan. The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported an unemployment rate of 4.4% in December, below the estimate of 4.5%. In addition, it revised November's unemployment rate to 4.5% from 4.6%.

JPMorgan Sees Core CPI Staying Above 3% in 2026

JPMorgan believes that the core Consumer Price Index (CPI), which excludes volatile food and energy prices, will stay above 3% this year alongside jobs and gross domestic product (GDP) growth. Lower rates could fuel inflation, as they decrease borrowing costs and stimulate spending.

The firm's forecast is at odds with investor expectations, with CME's FedWatch tool pricing in a 32.6% chance of two rate cuts by year-end and a 25.4% chance of one cut.
